concentration.headline: Moderately concentrated — one leading city
concentration.body: Manila leads Philippines's urban system with 18.1% of tracked urban population across 1579 cities — significant but not overwhelming. Secondary cities retain meaningful economic weight alongside the capital.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United Kingdom, Italy, Canada
concentration.contrast: Unlike: USA, Japan (more distributed) or Thailand, Argentina (more dominant)

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Manila & Caloocan City
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Manila and Caloocan City (6 km apart, combined population 26,583,584). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 8 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Philippines sits between distributed and dominant: Manila leads with 18.1% but doesn't overwhelm. Gravity clusters tightly around Manila → Caloocan City (8 km avg) — a strong sub-regional core coexists with the national leader, creating a layered urban hierarchy.
